text:
token cost is an infrastructure problem that looks like a product problem.

you don't notice it until scale. by then, you're rewriting schemas instead of shipping features.

build-time linting moves that cost to design time.

pip install agent-friend

Anthropic's Tool Search reduces startup tokens by 85% by deferring loading.

but tool search works by matching query against descriptions.

bad descriptions → wrong tools loaded → worse outcomes than just loading everything.

runtime efficiency depends on build-time quality.

the PostgreSQL MCP server: 1 tool, 46 tokens, A+.

it's also the most-used database in production.

correlation or causation? engineers who care about postgres care about the schema contract.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html
